name: Make
tagline: Visual automation platform for complex workflows
description: Advanced no-code automation with visual scenario builder
pros: Visual scenario builder with unlimited branching and loops
2,000+ app connectors with granular trigger/action options
Built-in data store for operational databases
Parallel processing and queue management for enterprise throughput
Version history and rollback for automation scenarios
cons: 2,000+ connectors vs Zapier's 7,000+ limits integration reach
Scenario builder learning curve steeper than Zapier's linear wizard
Operations-based pricing less predictable than Zapier's task model

content.title: Executive Summary
content.body: Advanced no-code automation with visual scenario builder Make earns a 4.3/5 rating from 4,800 user reviews.

content.title: TL;DR
content.body: Make is a leading automation platform rated 4.3/5 across 4,800 reviews. Pricing starts at Free–$29/mo. Best for Power Users and Operations Teams.

content.title: Rating Overview
content.body: Make scores 4.3/5 overall with ease of use (3.9/5), features (4.5/5), performance (4.3/5), support (3.9/5), value (4.3/5).

content.title: Quick Verdict
content.body: Make earns a 4.3/5 rating recommended for Power Users and Operations Teams. Delivers strong feature depth with functional design.

content.title: Company Background
content.body: Make was founded in 2016 in Prague, Czechia, serving 1M+ users customers with 300+ employees.

content.title: Product Overview
content.body: Make is a visual automation platform for complex workflows. Provides 18 features across Core, Advanced, Integrations, Analytics, Productivity, Security categories. Mobile on iOS and Android. API available.

content.title: Key Features
content.body: Essential automation features:

content.title: Feature Deep Dive
content.body: Scenario Builder. Visual drag-and-drop workflow. Templates. Pre-built automation recipes. Data Store. Built-in key-value storage.

content.title: AI Features
content.body: Make AI includes AI scenario builder, Data mapping suggestions.

content.title: API & Automation
content.body: Make provides a comprehensive API for custom integration and workflow automation.

content.title: Security
content.body: Make holds SOC 2 Type II, GDPR certifications with TLS 1.2+ and AES-256 encryption.

content.title: Compliance
content.body: Make complies with GDPR, CCPA regulations.

content.title: Integrations
content.body: Integrates with Slack, Google Workspace, Salesforce, HubSpot, Notion, Jira, GitHub, Shopify, and 2+ more.

content.title: Pricing
content.body: Make uses freemium with operation-based tiers. Free tier available. Plans from Free–$29/mo.

content.title: Pros
content.body: Make advantages:
items: Visual scenario builder with unlimited branching and loops
2,000+ app connectors with granular trigger/action options
Built-in data store for operational databases
Parallel processing and queue management for enterprise throughput

content.title: Cons
content.body: Considerations:
items: 2,000+ connectors vs Zapier's 7,000+ limits integration reach
Scenario builder learning curve steeper than Zapier's linear wizard
Operations-based pricing less predictable than Zapier's task model

content.title: Performance
content.body: Strong performance with fast load times.

content.title: Ease of Use
content.body: Moderate learning curve with comprehensive documentation.

content.title: User Experience
content.body: Functional organized interface.

content.title: Customer Support
content.body: Support rated 3.9/5 via email and knowledge base.

content.title: Industry Fit
content.body: Best for Automation and Productivity with Power Users and Operations Teams teams.

content.title: Team Size Fit
content.body: Make accommodates teams of all sizes with generous free tier. Best ROI for 5-50 person teams.

content.title: Real Use Cases
content.body: A power users team uses Make to streamline automation workflows and improve team productivity.

content.title: Competitor Analysis
content.body: Make competes with other platforms. Primary differentiators: feature depth and pricing.

content.title: Alternatives
content.body: Consider alternatives based on specific team requirements.

content.title: Migration Guide
content.body: Migration rated moderate complexity. Budget 2-4 weeks for full transition. Parallel testing recommended.

content.title: Buying Advice
content.body: Evaluate: feature alignment, total cost at Free–$29/mo, migration effort (2-4 weeks), and real-data trial.

content.title: Final Verdict
content.body: Make earns 4.3/5. Strong choice for Power Users and Operations Teams. Recommended for teams prioritizing automation.

faqs.question: What is Make best used for?
faqs.answer: Make is best used for advanced no-code automation with visual scenario builder.

faqs.question: How much does Make cost?
faqs.answer: Make uses freemium with operation-based tiers starting at Free–$29/mo. Free tier available.

faqs.question: Does Make integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, integrates with Slack, Google Workspace, Salesforce, HubSpot, Notion. And 5+ more.

faqs.answer: Yes, with a generous free tier. Freemium with operation-based tiers scales with team growth.

faqs.answer: Make uses freemium with operation-based tiers from Free–$29/mo, with free tier available.

faqs.answer: Yes, holds SOC 2 Type II, GDPR certifications with encryption.

author: PilotStack Team
learningCurve: Minimal — most users can get started within an hour
whoShouldUse: Make is perfect for freelancers and independent professionals who need an affordable, easy-to-use automation tool. Its simplicity and core feature set make it ideal for solo practitioners.
whoShouldNotUse: Make may not be suitable for large enterprises with complex compliance requirements or teams needing extensive customization options. Organizations with more than 500 users may find the pricing less competitive at scale.
